hero.title: Where Culture, Business and People Meet.

why.title: Why MaWePolo?
why.text: MaWePolo connects the Veneto region in Italy and the Małopolska region in Poland, two regions distant yet remarkably similar. Through this portal, we bring together exceptional opportunities for development and cooperation, bridging cultural, business, and tourism potential.

hero.title: Discover Małopolska
hero.description: Małopolska is one of Poland’s most dynamic regions, known for its rich cultural heritage, thriving tourism, and the historic city of Kraków.

cities.title: Tarnów
cities.text: A charming city with Renaissance architecture.

cities.title: Nowy Sącz
cities.text: A gateway to the mountains and wooden architecture.

cities.title: Oświęcim
cities.text: A city of memory and reflection.

cities.title: Wieliczka
cities.text: Home to the UNESCO‑listed Salt Mine.

culture.title: Art
culture.text: Sacred art, wooden churches, folk costumes.

culture.title: UNESCO Sites
culture.text: Kraków Old Town, Wieliczka Salt Mine, Wooden Churches.

hero.title: Discover Veneto
hero.description: Veneto is one of Northern Italy’s most influential regions, renowned for Venice, strong manufacturing districts, world‑class wine production, and a unique blend of culture, innovation, and tourism.

cities.title: Venezia
cities.text: A small gem crossed by canals and medieval arcades.

cities.title: Verona
cities.text: A gateway to the mountains, wooden architecture, and rich regional traditions.

cities.title: Treviso
cities.text: A small gem crossed by canals and medieval arcades.

cities.title: Padua
cities.text: A historic university city with ancient traditions.

cities.title: Vicenza
cities.text: The city of Palladio, Renaissance elegance, UNESCO heritage.

cities.title: Belluno
cities.text: Gateway to the Dolomites, pristine nature and spectacular mountain landscapes.

cities.title: Rovigo
cities.text: Between the Po and Adige rivers, medieval history and the calm atmosphere of Polesine.

territory.title: Villages
territory.text: Asolo, Marostica, Bassano del Grappa: historic villages, medieval castles, and unique landscapes.

territory.title: Landscapes
territory.text: Dolomites, Lake Garda, Adriatic coast: enchanting mountains, lakes, hills, and plains.

gastronomy.title: Food & Beverages
gastronomy.text: Discover the soul of Italian cuisine — from traditional dishes to modern interpretations.

gastronomy.title: Products
gastronomy.text: Discover the unique products of Veneto — from traditional crafts to modern innovations.

culture.title: Art
culture.text: Renaissance frescoes, sacred interiors, and timeless landscapes shaped by history.

culture.title: UNESCO Sites
culture.text: Venezia, Padova, Dolomiti: world heritage sites.

economy.title: Craft
economy.text: Ceramics, glassmaking, wood carving, textiles, and artisan traditions.

economy.title: Businesses
economy.text: Industry, design, agriculture, and craftsmanship shaping a diverse economy.

malopolska-detail-city-krakow.description: Kraków is the cultural heart of Poland — a city of kings, scholars, artists, and centuries of history.

blocks.title: Wawel Castle
blocks.text: A symbol of Polish heritage overlooking the Vistula River.

blocks.title: Main Market Square
blocks.text: One of Europe’s largest medieval squares.

blocks.title: Kazimierz District
blocks.text: A historic Jewish quarter full of cafés and galleries.

malopolska-detail-city-tarnow.description: Tarnów is a warm, elegant city with Renaissance architecture and a beautifully preserved old town.

blocks.title: Renaissance Old Town
blocks.text: Colorful façades and arcades.

blocks.title: Cultural Heritage
blocks.text: Museums and monuments reflect a multicultural past.

blocks.title: Sunny Climate
blocks.text: One of Poland’s warmest cities.

malopolska-detail-city-nowy-sacz.description: Nowy Sącz is a gateway to the mountains, wooden architecture, and rich regional traditions.

blocks.title: Wooden Architecture
blocks.text: Open-air museums and historic churches.

blocks.title: Mountain Landscapes
blocks.text: Beskid trails and peaceful nature.

blocks.title: Regional Traditions
blocks.text: Folk costumes, music, and cuisine.

malopolska-detail-city-zakopane.description: Zakopane is the winter capital of Poland, known for wooden villas and mountain culture.

blocks.title: Tatra Mountains

blocks.title: Highlander Culture
blocks.text: Wooden houses, music, and cuisine.

blocks.title: Krupówki Street
blocks.text: Shops, cafés, and local specialties.

malopolska-detail-city-oswiecim.description: Oświęcim is a city of memory and reflection, home to the Auschwitz‑Birkenau Memorial.

malopolska-detail-city-bochnia.description: Bochnia is one of Poland’s oldest cities, known for its historic salt mine.

blocks.title: Bochnia Salt Mine

blocks.text: Traditional wooden houses preserved for generations.

blocks.title: Tatra Mountains
blocks.text: Poland’s highest peaks.

blocks.title: Pieniny & Dunajec Gorge
blocks.text: A spectacular river canyon.

blocks.title: Beskids

blocks.title: Oscypek
blocks.text: Smoked sheep cheese.

blocks.title: Pierogi
blocks.text: Poland’s iconic dumplings.

blocks.title: Kwaśnica
blocks.text: A hearty mountain soup.

malopolska-detail-gastronomy-products.description: Local crafts, wooden carvings, lacework, pottery, and regional specialties.

blocks.title: Wooden Crafts
blocks.text: Hand‑carved decorations and ornaments.

blocks.title: Lacework & Textiles

blocks.title: Pottery

malopolska-detail-culture-art.description: Małopolska’s art spans sacred architecture, folk traditions, and centuries of craftsmanship.

blocks.title: Wooden Churches
blocks.text: UNESCO‑listed wooden churches.

blocks.title: Folk Costumes
blocks.text: Colorful regional outfits.

blocks.title: Sacred Art
blocks.text: Altarpieces and sculptures preserved in churches.

malopolska-detail-unesco-unesco-sites.description: Małopolska hosts several UNESCO World Heritage sites.

blocks.title: Kraków Old Town
blocks.text: A historic center full of monuments.

blocks.title: Wieliczka Salt Mine
blocks.text: An underground world of chambers and sculptures.

blocks.title: Wooden Churches
blocks.text: A unique collection of wooden sacral architecture.

blocks.title: Woodwork
blocks.text: Traditional carving techniques.

blocks.title: Textiles
blocks.text: Hand‑woven fabrics and embroidered garments.

blocks.title: Artisan Workshops
blocks.text: Local workshops preserve traditional skills.

malopolska-detail-economy-businesses.description: Małopolska’s economy blends tourism, technology, education, and local enterprises.

blocks.title: Tourism
blocks.text: Cities and mountains attract millions.

blocks.title: Local Enterprises
blocks.text: Small and medium businesses form the backbone of the economy.

veneto-detail-city-venezia.description: Venezia is a city unlike any other — a labyrinth of canals, bridges, and centuries‑old palaces rising from the water.

blocks.title: City of Canals and Bridges
blocks.text: Gondolas, waterways, and iconic bridges shape a unique urban landscape.

blocks.title: Piazzas and Venetian Life
blocks.text: St. Mark’s Square is the heart of the city, full of life and history.

blocks.title: Art, Light, and Heritage
blocks.text: Palaces and churches preserve masterpieces of Venetian art.

veneto-detail-city-verona.description: Verona blends Roman heritage, medieval streets, and romantic riverfront views.

blocks.title: Roman and Medieval Heritage
blocks.text: Arena di Verona and ancient towers reveal centuries of history.

blocks.title: Piazzas and City Rhythm
blocks.text: Markets and cafés animate the city’s historic squares.

blocks.title: Riverside Views and Romance
blocks.text: Stone bridges and neighborhoods create a timeless atmosphere.

veneto-detail-city-treviso.description: Treviso is an intimate city of canals, arcades, and quiet piazzas.

blocks.title: City of Water and Arcades
blocks.text: Canals and elegant arcades define Treviso’s charm.

blocks.title: Piazzas and Daily Life
blocks.text: The city’s heart beats in its lively squares.

blocks.title: Flavors of the Territory
blocks.text: Prosecco hills and local cuisine enrich the region.

veneto-detail-city-padua.description: Padua is a historic university city shaped by science, faith, and art.

blocks.title: Ancient University Tradition
blocks.text: Founded in 1222, the university hosted great minds like Galileo.

blocks.title: Sacred Art and Pilgrimage
blocks.text: The Basilica of Saint Anthony and Giotto’s frescoes attract visitors worldwide.

blocks.title: Squares and Markets
blocks.text: Historic markets fill the city with color and life.

veneto-detail-city-vicenza.description: Vicenza is the city of Palladio, where architecture becomes a living stage.

blocks.title: Palladian Heritage
blocks.text: Villas and palaces shaped European architecture.

blocks.title: Teatro Olimpico
blocks.text: A masterpiece of Renaissance perspective and design.

blocks.title: Elegant City Life
blocks.text: Boutiques and cafés enrich the historic center.

veneto-detail-city-belluno.description: Belluno is the gateway to the Dolomites, suspended between nature and history.

blocks.title: Historic Center
blocks.text: Elegant palaces and churches overlook mountain landscapes.

veneto-detail-city-rovigo.description: Rovigo lies between rivers and reclaimed lands, offering quiet rhythms and historic traces.

blocks.title: Between Rivers and Land
blocks.text: A landscape shaped by waterways and fields.

blocks.title: Medieval Traces
blocks.text: Towers and churches tell centuries of history.

blocks.title: Quiet Atmosphere

blocks.title: Asolo
blocks.text: The city of a hundred horizons.

blocks.title: Marostica
blocks.text: Famous for its living chess game.

blocks.title: Bassano del Grappa
blocks.text: Wooden bridge, grappa, and mountain views.

veneto-detail-territory-landscapes.description: From Dolomites to Lake Garda and the Adriatic coast, Veneto offers diverse landscapes.

blocks.title: Dolomites

blocks.title: Adriatic Coast

blocks.title: Radicchio Rosso di Treviso
blocks.text: A unique bitter‑sweet vegetable.

blocks.title: Soppressa Veneta
blocks.text: Traditional countryside salami.

blocks.title: Asiago DOP

veneto-detail-culture-art.description: Veneto’s art spans centuries, from sacred frescoes to contemporary creativity.

blocks.title: Renaissance and Sacred Art
blocks.text: Churches and palaces preserve masterpieces.

blocks.title: Living Culture

veneto-detail-culture-unesco-sites.description: Veneto hosts several UNESCO sites of exceptional cultural and natural value.

blocks.title: Venice and its Lagoon
blocks.text: A unique city on water.

blocks.title: Dolomites
blocks.text: A natural monument of global significance.

blocks.text: Venetian glass is world‑renowned.

blocks.title: Wood and Furniture

blocks.title: Agriculture and Wine
blocks.text: Vineyards and orchards shape the region.

blocks.title: Tourism and Services
blocks.text: Cities, mountains, lakes, and coastlines attract millions.
